scope: Real editor-host acceptance smoke for VS Code Extension Development Host and a disposable Obsidian vault/plugin, using mock provider first and one bounded pi-backed VS Code request after mock success.

summary.result: VS Code real Extension Development Host smoke passed for mock provider and for one bounded pi-backed synthetic TypeScript request: ghost text was visible in screenshots and Tab acceptance changed the disposable file. Obsidian GUI tooling was available and a disposable vault launched under Xvfb, but the Obsidian client could not be loaded as a real plugin from the documented local-source setup; therefore Obsidian ghost text/Tab acceptance remains blocked and not accepted.
summary.acceptance_statement: Do not claim full end-to-end editor GUI MVP acceptance yet. VS Code is accepted for this smoke slice; Obsidian is blocked by plugin packaging/runtime module-resolution defects before any broker request is made.

environment.display: :0 was available, but smokes used xvfb-run for isolated virtual GUI sessions

tooling.xdotool: not installed; automation used Python ctypes with X11 XTEST

commands_run.result: completed before writes. It showed pre-existing unrelated deleted legacy reports/orchestration/* entries plus untracked orchestration/archive/worker paths; these were preserved.
passed. Rust workspace tests and doc tests passed across autocomplete-addons, autocomplete-core, autocomplete-protocol, autocomplete-provider-pi, and autocomplete-server.
passed. 9 Node tests passed across shared protocol, VS Code host glue, Obsidian host glue, stale cancellation, and provider-boundary checks.

commands_run.command: command -v code obsidian xvfb-run xclip pi; code --version; pi --version
commands_run.result: passed for required commands. code reported 1.120.0; pi reported 0.75.5. Obsidian does not print a useful CLI --version, but smoke logs reported Obsidian 1.12.7.

passed for VS Code mock smoke. The script started a mock broker on 127.0.0.1:32148, launched an isolated VS Code Extension Development Host with --extensionDevelopmentPath clients/vscode, opened a disposable /tmp sample.ts, triggered inline suggestion via the command palette, captured visible ghost text, pressed Tab, saved, and verified file content changed to include `return n + 1`.

passed for one bounded VS Code pi-backed smoke. The script started a pi broker on 127.0.0.1:32151 with provider=openai-codex, model=gpt-5.5, thinking=minimal, timeout_ms=10000 and client deadlineMs=12000, used only a synthetic TypeScript file, triggered inline suggestion, waited 13 s, captured visible ghost text, pressed Tab, saved, and verified file content changed to `return n + 1;`.

failed/blocking for documented Obsidian local-source setup. The script started a mock broker on 127.0.0.1:32149, created a disposable vault, symlinked clients/obsidian and clients/protocol exactly as docs/client-setup.md describes, trusted the vault, attempted to enable the plugin, and Obsidian displayed `Failed to load plugin "autocomplete-alternative"`. No broker request was observed and the note stayed unchanged.

failed/blocking diagnostic. With a temporary root main.js wrapper added only inside the disposable plugin folder, plugin load still failed before any broker request. The wrapper captured `Error: Cannot find module 'obsidian'` from clients/obsidian/src/main.js, indicating the unbundled source is not directly loadable as a real Obsidian plugin artifact.

editor_host.ghost_text_evidence: Screenshot /tmp/mvp071-vscode-0ctulj9_/vscode-before-tab.png shows grey inline text ` n + 1` after `return` before Tab; /tmp/mvp071-vscode-0ctulj9_/vscode-after-tab.png was captured after acceptance.
editor_host.tab_acceptance_evidence: After pressing Tab and saving, sample.ts contained the mock insert text at the cursor.

editor_host.ghost_text_evidence: Screenshot /tmp/mvp071-vscode-pi-cuxvdc3m/vscode-pi-before-tab.png shows grey inline text ` n + 1;` after `return` before Tab.
editor_host.tab_acceptance_evidence: After pressing Tab and saving, sample.ts contained the pi insert text at the cursor.

notes: The editor-path smoke used a fixed wait after trigger rather than direct provider latency instrumentation, so it proves bounded end-to-end success but not precise latency.
The pi request used only synthetic TypeScript content in /tmp and the broker's no-tools/no-context-files pi isolation path.

documented_setup_attempt.plugin_setup: ln -s $PWD/clients/obsidian <vault>/.obsidian/plugins/autocomplete-alternative; ln -s $PWD/clients/protocol <vault>/.obsidian/plugins/protocol; trusted the vault; attempted to enable plugin in Community plugins settings. A broker data.json setting attempt was not material to the result because the plugin failed before loading.
documented_setup_attempt.observed_ui: Obsidian 1.12.7 displayed a toast: `Failed to load plugin "autocomplete-alternative"` after enabling the plugin.
documented_setup_attempt.evidence:
Screenshot /tmp/mvp071-obsidian-docs-smdnr6k2/docs-after-enable-click.png shows the failed-load toast. Broker log remained empty and note.md remained `# Smoke

Today I measured` after Tab.
documented_setup_attempt.likely_primary_blocker: clients/obsidian is not a loadable Obsidian plugin artifact as documented because the plugin root has no main.js; it contains manifest.json, package.json, src/main.js, styles.css, and tests. Obsidian plugin loading expects a root main.js artifact.

diagnostic_shim_attempt.setup: A temporary disposable-vault-only root main.js wrapper was added to require the repository source and capture load errors; no repository files were changed.
diagnostic_shim_attempt.captured_error:
Error: Cannot find module 'obsidian'
Require stack:
- /home/abhmul/dev/autocomplete-alternative/clients/obsidian/src/main.js
- electron/js2c/renderer_init
diagnostic_shim_attempt.evidence: Captured at /tmp/mvp071-obsidian-shim-8ze0aepb/plugin-error.txt; screenshot /tmp/mvp071-obsidian-shim-8ze0aepb/shim-after-enable-click.png again shows `Failed to load plugin "autocomplete-alternative"`. No broker request was observed and note.md remained unchanged.
diagnostic_shim_attempt.interpretation: Even with a root entrypoint shim, the unbundled source is not directly loadable in the actual Obsidian runtime. The Obsidian client needs a real plugin build/bundle or a loader-compatible root main.js that resolves `obsidian` and CodeMirror imports correctly.

pi_provider.reason: Obsidian mock provider smoke did not reach plugin load or broker request; attempting a real pi request would not test editor ghost text and would waste a bounded provider call.

manual_steps_needed_after_fix: Create or build a real Obsidian plugin artifact under .obsidian/plugins/autocomplete-alternative with root manifest.json, main.js, styles.css, and any bundled dependencies or Obsidian-compatible externals.
Use a disposable vault, write .obsidian/community-plugins.json or enable via Settings → Community plugins, trust the vault, and enable Autocomplete Alternative without a failed-load toast.

Start the mock broker, set the plugin Broker URL to http://127.0.0.1:32145 or the chosen port, open a Markdown note, place the cursor in synthetic text, wait for ghost text, press Tab, and verify the note content changes.
Only after mock passes, repeat once with a bounded pi broker and synthetic Markdown context.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Run existing cargo/npm tests first as a sanity check.
evidence: npm test --prefix clients passed with 9 tests.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: VS Code client renders inline ghost text and accepts with Tab in an actual Extension Development Host against mock provider.
evidence: Xvfb-launched VS Code 1.120.0 Extension Development Host loaded clients/vscode via --extensionDevelopmentPath.
Screenshot before Tab shows mock ghost text ` n + 1` after `return`.
After Tab and save, disposable sample.ts contained `return n + 1`.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: VS Code client can complete one bounded pi-backed request if safe and practical.
evidence: Broker pi health returned status ok with provider name pi:gpt-5.5.
Screenshot before Tab shows pi ghost text ` n + 1;` after `return`.
After Tab and save, disposable sample.ts contained `return n + 1;`.
The run used provider timeout 10000 ms, client deadline 12000 ms, outer timeout 150 s, and synthetic /tmp TypeScript content only.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Obsidian client renders ghost text and accepts with Tab in a disposable vault against mock provider.
evidence: Obsidian 1.12.7 launched under Xvfb with a disposable vault and trusted plugins.
Documented symlink setup failed to load the plugin with UI toast `Failed to load plugin "autocomplete-alternative"`.
No autocomplete broker request was observed and note.md did not change after Tab.
Diagnostic wrapper captured `Cannot find module 'obsidian'` when requiring the unbundled source.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Obsidian client can complete one bounded pi-backed request if mock succeeds.
evidence: Not safe/practical because Obsidian mock smoke failed before plugin load and before any broker request.

blockers.title: Obsidian local-source client is not a loadable plugin artifact
blockers.details: The documented symlink setup points Obsidian at clients/obsidian, but that directory has no root main.js. Enabling the plugin in Obsidian 1.12.7 fails with a `Failed to load plugin` toast before any broker request.

blockers.title: Obsidian source needs bundling or runtime-compatible module resolution
blockers.details: A disposable-vault-only root main.js wrapper reached clients/obsidian/src/main.js but failed with `Cannot find module 'obsidian'`, so simply exposing src/main.js as root main.js is insufficient. A real plugin bundle or loader-compatible artifact is needed for actual Obsidian runtime smoke.

recommended_follow_ups.title: Add an Obsidian plugin build/local artifact task
recommended_follow_ups.details: Create a root main.js artifact for clients/obsidian, bundle or externalize Obsidian/CodeMirror imports correctly, update docs/client-setup.md, then rerun MVP-071 Obsidian mock and pi smokes.

recommended_follow_ups.title: Make editor smoke automation durable
recommended_follow_ups.details: Check in or document a minimal opt-in smoke harness for VS Code and Obsidian using Xvfb/XTEST and disposable files, while keeping real pi calls explicitly bounded.

safety_notes: All editor documents/vaults used for smoke were disposable /tmp paths with synthetic content only.
The only real pi editor-path request sent synthetic TypeScript prefix/suffix from /tmp/sample.ts; no repository content or sensitive files were sent.
Did not read prompt-buffer.md, repo tmp/, archive contents, or unrelated ~/dev directories.
Pre-existing unrelated worktree changes were preserved.

One smoke-script attempt briefly created untracked clients/obsidian/data.json through a symlinked disposable-vault plugin path; it was removed before checkpointing and is not present in final git status.
worktree_notes: Final intended repository changes for this task are reports/orchestration/mvp-2026-05-28/handoffs/MVP-071-real-editor-host-smoke.json and checkpoint daily-note bookkeeping.
Temporary automation scripts and screenshots were left under /tmp paths named mvp071-* and were not added to the repository.
